text: The patient is placed in a supine position with the arm extended above the head.
The patient is deliberately intubated in the right main stem in order to collapse the left lung.
The incision is made along the fifth rib from the sternum and extended to the posterior clavicular line.
Blunt tip scissors are used to cut into the intercostal muscle just over the fifth rib.
The rib spreader is placed and the ribs are separated.
The left upper lobe of the lung is moved in order to expose the pericardium.
A gentle incision is made above the phrenic nerve in order to expose the heart.
The rest of the pericardium can be spread manually.
This maneuver will decompress the pericardial tamponade and provides access to the heart for repair and cardiac massage.
